evidence.text: File contents were transformed with symmetric encryption; encrypted extensions appeared rapidly.
Renamed large numbers of user files with random extensions after modification.
Dropped a ransom note demanding payment for file recovery.
Created a scheduled task for execution at user logon.
Contacted a remote server over HTTPS to report host identifiers and encryption status.
Repeated file encryption observed; invoked CryptEncrypt on many user documents.
Renamed large numbers of user files with random extensions after modification.
Dropped a ransom note demanding payment for file recovery.
Created a scheduled task for execution at user logon.
Outbound HTTPS beaconing to a hard-coded IP address on a non-standard port.
File contents were transformed with symmetric encryption; encrypted extensions appeared rapidly.
Created new encrypted copies and removed originals across user directories.
Created README files instructing victims to pay for decryption keys.
Created registry Run key HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\sysupdater.
Issued periodic HTTPS requests to a fixed endpoint consistent with C2 telemetry.
